claim: Replicating Bowern & Lindemann 2021's MATTR (Moving Average Type-Token Ratio, 2000-word window per Gheuens 2019) methodology on Brain-V's hand-separated Voynich corpus reveals whether Hand A specifically ranks closer to B&L's 'closest' cluster (Germanic, Iranian, Romance) or their 'least resembles' cluster (Turkic, Dravidian, Kartvelian — the agglutinative families). This resolves the CONTRADICTS_B&L flag on finding #10 (agglutinative typology).
evidence_for: B&L 2021 LV §3.4 p.15: 'Another statistic which is useful for determining the lexical diversity of a text is the type-token ratio... Gheuens (2019) introduced the Moving Average Type Token ratio (MATTR) index... Gheuens (2019) examined MATTR in Voynichese compared with a sample of language texts, and concluded that 2000 is an ideal window. We have also found 2000 words to be a good window for comparing MATTR in language families.'

B&L 2021 LV §3.4 p.16 (Figure 9 reference): 'The Voynich texts are once again in the medium range, and closest to Iranian, Germanic, and Romance.' Their conclusion: 'Voynich most closely resembles the averages for Germanic and Iranian, and least resembles those for Turkic, Dravidian, and Kartvelian.'

B&L's analysis was on whole-manuscript or whole-language (A, B, Full) aggregates. They did not publish hand-separated MATTR comparisons against reference families. If Hand A differs from the aggregate due to composition effects, Brain-V's hand-separated comparison could reveal rankings B&L's aggregate masked.

Methodology lesson 3 (sanity-check external baselines on the original corpus) applies: Brain-V's agglutinative-match finding (H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-EXPANSION-01) uses a different typological methodology (Brain-V's M1-M5 profile) than B&L's MATTR. Running B&L's actual methodology on Brain-V's data is the direct replication their framework requires.
evidence_against: UD treebank corpora used as reference families are annotated subsets of various registers (news, fiction, academic), not strictly comparable to the Voynich manuscript register. B&L's exact reference corpora are not specified in detail. Family-mean comparisons may have register-dependent noise.
Tamil TTB (6,329 tokens) and Telugu MTG (5,082 tokens) are marginal for 2000-word window MATTR (only 3-4 windows). Dravidian family mean will be less reliable than Germanic or Romance means.

Kartvelian (Georgian) corpus not available in Brain-V's fetched reference set — one of B&L's three 'least resembles' agglutinative families is missing from this comparison. Results still informative for Turkic and Dravidian comparisons.

If Hand A MATTR lands between Germanic and agglutinative family means (e.g. 0.65 when Germanic mean is 0.60 and agglutinative is 0.70), the test won't cleanly resolve the contradiction; a PARTIAL verdict with interpretation caveats.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE — EXACT REPLICATION OF B&L 2021 MATTR METHODOLOGY.

  DATA:
    Voynichese (primary): Hand A all tokens, Hand B all tokens (from voynich-corpus.json filtering by currier_language).
    Voynichese (hand+section controlled): Hand A herbal tokens, Hand B herbal tokens.
    Reference corpora (from raw/corpus/reference-corpora/):
      Germanic: UD_English-EWT (204,572 tokens), UD_German-GSD (263,777)
      Romance: UD_Spanish-GSD (382,444), UD_French-GSD (354,648)
      Iranian: UD_Persian-Seraji (121,067)
      Semitic: UD_Arabic-PADT (223,881), UD_Hebrew-HTB (136,501)
      Vasconic: UD_Basque-BDT (39,456 content, ~72k total)
      Uralic (= B&L's Finno-Ugric): UD_Hungarian-Szeged (~130k)
      Turkic: UD_Turkish-IMST (~234k)
      Dravidian: UD_Tamil-TTB (6,329) + UD_Telugu-MTG (5,082)
      Constructed: Esperanto Wikipedia plaintext (~60k tokens)
    Missing: Kartvelian (Georgian). Flagged as a gap; not blocker.

  TOKENISATION: tokens are whitespace-separated words as extracted from each corpus. For Voynichese, tokens are raw EVA words (no ligature tokenisation). For UD corpora, tokens are the FORM column (one per row, MWT ranges skipped).

  MATTR COMPUTATION (LOCKED, per Covington & McFall 2010 / Gheuens 2019 / B&L 2021):
    Window W = 2000 words.
    For each starting position i = 0 to N-W (step 1):
      window = tokens[i : i+W]
      TTR(i) = |distinct types in window| / W
    MATTR = mean of TTR(i) across all positions.
    If N < W + 1: corpus has insufficient tokens for a stable MATTR; mark LOW_POWER and compute what's possible (but do not compare to reference family means).

  LANGUAGE FAMILY MEANS (lock family assignment):
    Germanic_mean = mean(MATTR(English), MATTR(German))
    Romance_mean = mean(MATTR(Spanish), MATTR(French))
    Iranian_mean = MATTR(Persian)
    Semitic_mean = mean(MATTR(Arabic), MATTR(Hebrew))
    Vasconic = MATTR(Basque)   (family of 1 since Basque is isolate)
    Uralic = MATTR(Hungarian)
    Turkic = MATTR(Turkish)
    Dravidian_mean = mean(MATTR(Tamil), MATTR(Telugu))  — flag Tamil/Telugu as marginal

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    Compute MATTR for Hand A (and Hand B) and compute absolute distance from each language family mean.
    Determine which family Hand A is CLOSEST to.
    Classify the result into one of four bins:

    BIN 1 — B&L CONFIRMED on Hand A:
      Hand A MATTR is closest to Germanic / Romance / Iranian / Semitic means (B&L's 'closest' cluster).
      AND Hand A distance to closest agglutinative family (Turkic / Uralic / Vasconic / Dravidian) is > 2x its distance to closest non-agglutinative.
      Implication: B&L's conclusion holds even under hand separation; Brain-V's Basque finding is methodology-dependent (M1-M5 vs MATTR give different answers).

    BIN 2 — B&L CONTRADICTED on Hand A:
      Hand A MATTR is closest to Turkic / Uralic / Vasconic / Dravidian means.
      AND Hand A distance to closest non-agglutinative family is > 2x its distance to closest agglutinative.
      Implication: B&L's aggregate result masks hand-specific typology. Brain-V's Basque finding is a genuine extension: hand-controlled MATTR reveals agglutinative-congruence that whole-language aggregate misses.

    BIN 3 — AMBIGUOUS:
      Hand A MATTR is between family clusters, or closest distance ratio is less than 2x.
      Implication: MATTR methodology on Hand A doesn't cleanly resolve either way. Report as methodology-dependent inconclusive.

    BIN 4 — LOW_POWER:
      Hand A has <= 2000 tokens (not the case — Hand A has ~11k) OR reference family n is too small for reliable mean (e.g. Dravidian with only Tamil+Telugu each <10k tokens).

  PARALLEL REPORTING:
    Same classification for Hand B (expected to also fall somewhere).
    Same classification for Hand A herbal and Hand B herbal (hand+section controlled).
    Voynich full manuscript MATTR (reproducing B&L's aggregate number).

  COMPARISON TO BRAIN-V PRIOR AGGLUTINATIVE FINDING:
    H-BV-AGGLUTINATIVE-EXPANSION-01 used M1-M5 morphological profile and concluded Hand A is BASQUE_UNIQUELY_BEST.
    This test uses B&L's MATTR and will classify Hand A into BIN 1/2/3/4.
    Report side-by-side: which methodology gives which conclusion.
test_metric: MATTR (2000-word window) per corpus. Hand A distance to each family mean. Classification into bins.
test_threshold: BIN 1 (B&L CONFIRMED), BIN 2 (B&L CONTRADICTED), BIN 3 (AMBIGUOUS), BIN 4 (LOW_POWER).
tests_remaining: locked_mattr_reproduction: compute MATTR with 2000-word window on all corpora; compute family means; classify Hand A and Hand B
reasoning: Direct pipeline replication of B&L 2021's MATTR methodology on Brain-V's hand-separated data. Tests whether their aggregate conclusion (Voynichese closest to Germanic/Iranian/Romance, least like agglutinative families) holds when Hand A is analysed specifically. Outcome determines whether Brain-V's Basque finding (from M1-M5 profile methodology) is (a) compatible with B&L's framework under hand separation, or (b) a methodology artefact that MATTR doesn't support. This is the highest-stakes remaining test for v2 rewrite — the single item classified CONTRADICTS_B&L in the framework audit.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-18 direct replication of Bowern & Lindemann 2021 MATTR methodology, motivated by the Bowern-Lindemann framework audit flagging finding #10 (agglutinative typology) as CONTRADICTS_B&L.
